blocks.content: November. Cold in the mornings now.
They didn't talk about the dance. But things had shifted sideways into a place without a clear map, and both of them were navigating it without saying so.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Are you going to eat that.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You've been staring at it for ten minutes.
Eli looked between them. Looked at Savannah. Looked back at his own tray and said nothing, which was genuinely impressive.
He looked at her. She was already looking at her own tray. He ate his sandwich.
Savannah kicked Eli under the table. Eli took a very focused interest in his apple.
Same as always. Except everything was careful now, and they all knew it, and nobody said a word.
